title: The Whirlpool Galaxy comes alive | Space photo of the day for May 13, 2026
summary: The Whirlpool Galaxy (M51) is featured in a new image from the James Webb Space Telescope. The galaxy's stunning appearance is highlighted as NASA's Space Photo of the Day for May 13, 2026.

title: 30-mile-high clouds of acid on Venus are made by the largest 'hydraulic jump' in the solar system
summary: Scientists have discovered that a 3,700-mile-long cloud bank on Venus is formed by a massive 'hydraulic jump,' similar to how water spreads in a kitchen sink. This phenomenon creates the planet's 30-mile-high clouds of acid.

title: 'Like putting a microscope into the core of the sun': World's 1st space-based neutrino detector launches to orbit
summary: The world's first space-based neutrino detector has launched to orbit this month to study elusive neutrino particles that constantly bombard Earth. This mission is compared to 'putting a microscope into the core of the sun' due to its groundbreaking nature.

title: Cost estimate for 'Golden Dome' missile defense system balloons to $1.2 trillion
summary: The cost estimate for President Trump's 'Golden Dome' missile defense system has surged to $1.2 trillion. A new Congressional Budget Office report highlights the significant financial burden of developing and operating the system.

title: SpaceX's Starship V3 megarocket finally has a debut launch date. Here's when it will fly
summary: SpaceX has set May 19 as the debut launch date for its Starship Version 3, a new megarocket designed to support future moon and Mars missions.

title: Uranus and Neptune could be full of rocks, new study suggests
summary: A new study suggests that Uranus and Neptune may be composed more of rocks than ice, leading to the proposal of calling them 'minor giants.' The findings challenge traditional classifications of the ice giants.

title: Cowboy Space raises $275 million to launch AI data centers on brand-new rocket
summary: Cowboy Space raises $275 million to launch AI data centers in orbit using a new rocket. The funds will support the development and operation of these orbital data centers.

title: 'Cannibal stars,' AI and the Rubin Observatory could shed light on the mystery of dark energy. Here's how
summary: Scientists are using AI and Rubin Observatory data to rethink Type 1a supernova 'standard candles' in the search for dark energy's missing ingredients. This approach aims to uncover 'unknown unknowns' that could solve the cosmic puzzle.

title: Don't miss Mars, Saturn and the moon form a cosmic triangle before dawn on May 14
summary: On May 14, a rare celestial event will occur where Mars, Saturn, and the Moon form a cosmic triangle before dawn. This alignment will be visible as the Moon meets Saturn and Mars in the glow of the rising sun.

title: Watch SpaceX launch 6,500 pounds of cargo to the International Space Station on May 13
summary: SpaceX is scheduled to launch 6,500 pounds of cargo to the International Space Station on May 13. The Dragon cargo capsule's launch can be watched live.

title: NASA Langley Engineer Attends FAA Training
summary: NASA Langley engineer attends FAA training to address challenges in high-traffic airport communications, where spectrum and time constraints can lead to message loss during simultaneous transmissions.

title: Perseverance Stuns in New Selfie
summary: NASA's Perseverance rover captured a new selfie at the 'Lac de Charmes' site on Mars, composed of 61 images. The selfie shows the rover examining a rocky outcrop after creating an abrasion patch.

title: NASA’s Perseverance Rover Snaps Selfie in Mars’ Western Frontier
summary: NASA's Perseverance rover captured a selfie at Mars' 'Lac de Charmes' using 61 images, showcasing a rocky outcrop where it made an abrasion patch.

title: NASA’s Perseverance Captures Panorama at ‘Arbot’
summary: NASA's Perseverance rover captured a detailed panorama of the 'Arbot' area on Mars using its Mastcam-Z camera. The image, composed of 46 photos, provides a rich geological view during the rover's exploration beyond Jezero Crater.

title: NASA’s Perseverance Rover Snaps Westernmost Selfie
summary: NASA's Perseverance Mars rover captured a new selfie on March 11, 2026, during its deepest westward exploration beyond Jezero Crater. The image, stitched from 61 photos, shows the rover examining the 'Arethusa' rocky outcrop after creating a circular abrasion patch.

title: Hello Universe: NASA’s Next-Gen Space Processor Undergoes Testing
summary: NASA's High Performance Spaceflight Computing project is testing next-generation space processors to enhance computing power for spacecraft. The new chips aim to enable autonomous spacecraft and accelerate scientific discovery, addressing the limitations of older, reliable but less powerful processors.

title: I Am Artemis: Kathleen Harmon
summary: Kathleen Harmon, the Artemis II Mission Interface Manager for NASA's Deep Space Network, shares her journey from being inspired by Apollo launches as a child to playing a key role in NASA's Artemis program.

title: Australia’s Cloudy Beauty
summary: Australia's Victorian Alps experienced foggy conditions with an arch-shaped cloud drifting over Port Phillip Bay. This meteorological event occurred in the region known as Cloudy Beauty Valley.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4886-4892: Ingenuity and Perseverance, Curiosity Style
summary: Curiosity rover's recent activities on Mars included science operations inspired by the Ingenuity helicopter and Perseverance rover. The week's achievements were detailed in a blog post by Michelle Minitti, MAHLI Deputy Principal Investigator.

title: SpaceX CRS-34 resupply mission to ISS delayed 24 hours
summary: The SpaceX CRS-34 resupply mission to the International Space Station (ISS) has been delayed by 24 hours. This comes shortly after the Progress MS-34 spacecraft docked with the station.

title: Launch Preview: SpaceX and Chinese missions fill busy launch manifest
summary: SpaceX and China are preparing for a busy week of launches starting May 11, with a total of nine missions scheduled across both countries. The launches span various sectors including commercial, scientific, and government payloads.

title: Full Stack Flight 12 completes WDR
summary: Full Stack Flight 12 completed a Wet Dress Rehearsal (WDR) after Booster 19's successful static fire test. The event marks a significant milestone for the partner organization involved.

title: China prepares cargo, crew, and deep space missions, as commercial sector steps towards reusability
summary: China is preparing to launch both cargo and crew missions to its Tiangong space station while advancing its commercial sector towards reusability. The country is also focusing on deep space missions as part of its broader space program.

title: Pad 1 conversion ongoing, with Flight 12 nearing
summary: Pad 1 is undergoing conversion work, with Flight 12 approaching. The process follows the conclusion of Flight 11 six months prior.

title: Launch Preview: three launches — a calm before the storm?
summary: Three launches are scheduled, including two SpaceX Falcon 9 missions and one Chang Zheng 7 mission. This may precede a busier period in spaceflight activity.

title: SpaceX launches CAS500-2 and 45 other payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit
summary: SpaceX successfully launched its third rideshare mission of 2026 aboard a Falcon 9 rocket, carrying CAS500-2 and 45 other payloads to a Sun-synchronous orbit. This marks another milestone in SpaceX's rideshare program, expanding access to space for multiple customers.

title: SpaceX aims for mid-May Starship Flight 12 launch with revised trajectory
summary: SpaceX is targeting a mid-May launch for its 12th Starship flight test, featuring a revised trajectory. The company is preparing for the return of Starship launches after previous test flights.

title: Russia debuts new rocket with maiden Soyuz-5 launch
summary: Russia successfully conducted the maiden launch of its new Soyuz-5 rocket on Saturday. The debut flight marks a significant milestone for the country's space program.

title: Next NASA ISS astronaut, doctor discusses upcoming mission
summary: A doctor with experience in mass casualty events and overseas service has been selected as the next NASA astronaut for an ISS mission. The astronaut discusses preparations for the upcoming mission.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements. The publication marks five decades of the agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are helping the European Space Agency (ESA) test and develop materials to withstand the harsh conditions of space. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section relies on their innovative ideas to ensure mission success.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance accessibility and innovation in the space sector.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: The article discusses a critical test involving a parachute bake-out process, likely related to a space mission or spacecraft recovery system. This process is essential for ensuring the reliability of parachutes used in high-altitude or space environments.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: The ESA is sterilizing a 35-meter parachute for the ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission to ensure it is 10,000 times cleaner than a smartphone, preventing microbial contamination of Mars. The parachute, made of nylon and Kevlar, will slow the rover during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology is being developed to improve the handling and docking of spacecraft in space. This innovation aims to enhance the safety and efficiency of space missions.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has successfully completed space-like tests, remaining on schedule for a 2027 launch to search for terrestrial planets.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA invites SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual SME Forum 2026, held on June 8-9 in Paris, with an online preparatory session in April-May. The event aims to gather feedback on topics affecting small and medium-sized space sector enterprises.

title: Exclusive: LEAP Launches its First Rocket
summary: LEAP has successfully launched its first rocket, marking its entry into the space launch industry. This development introduces a new competitor in the commercial launch market.

title: CBO Puts $1.2T Price Tag on Golden Dome
summary: The Congressional Budget Office (CBO) estimates the cost of the Golden Dome program at $1.2 trillion, significantly higher than the $185 billion figure proposed by its leader, Gen. Michael Guetlein, in March.

title: Exobiosphere, Voyager Partner on ISS Mission
summary: Exobiosphere has partnered with Voyager to deploy an automated mini lab for high-throughput screening on the ISS. The Orbital High-Throughput Screening Device is designed to fit into standard mid-deck lockers aboard crewed or uncrewed space stations.

title: NASA’s Launch Director Pick Turns Political
summary: Brian Hughes, NASA's first senior launch operations director, previously served as the agency's chief of staff but left after Administrator Jared Isaacman took over. His new role is seen as a political move.

title: Star Catcher Closes $65M Series A
summary: Star Catcher has secured $65 million in a Series A funding round led by B Capital, with Shield Capital and Cerberus Ventures co-leading the investment.

title: Fenix Space Flies Tow-Launch Prototype
summary: Fenix Space has achieved a milestone by flying a tow-launch prototype, marking progress toward its space launch goals. The company is advancing its technology to facilitate future space missions.

title: Aetherflux Rebrands, Pivots Business—and Raises $275M
summary: Aetherflux has rebranded to Cowboy Space Corporation and raised $275 million to align with its new business direction.

title: SOCOM Taps SkyFi to Build Tactical EO Imagery Tools
summary: SOCOM has selected SkyFi to develop a prototype for a sovereign intelligence platform that will provide troops with faster and easier access to geospatial imagery. The platform aims to enhance tactical decision-making through improved satellite-based Earth observation tools.

title: Lunar Outpost Closes $30M Series B, Unveils New Rover
summary: Lunar Outpost raised $30 million in a Series B funding round, unveiling a new rover. The investment reflects investor interest in supporting NASA's lunar exploration plans.

title: Rocket Lab To Acquire Motiv Space Systems
summary: Rocket Lab will acquire space robotics company Motiv Space Systems, with the acquisition expected to close in Q2 and Motiv rebranded as Rocket Lab Robotics. The deal was announced alongside Rocket Lab's Q1 earnings.

title: Networks Business Propels Revenue Growth for SES, Operator Cancels 2 GEO Sats
summary: SES's Networks business drives revenue growth, leading the operator to cancel two geostationary orbit (GEO) satellites. The company's financial results align with analyst expectations.

title: AST SpaceMobile Confirms Target for 45 BlueBirds This Year, Despite Blue Origin Launch Failure
summary: AST SpaceMobile aims to deploy 45 BlueBird satellites in 2024 despite a recent Blue Origin launch failure. CEO Abel Avellan confirmed the target during an investor update.

title: Star Catcher Raises $65M Series A to Fuel In-Orbit Power Grid
summary: Star Catcher, led by former Made in Space and Redwire executive Andrew Rush, raised $65 million to develop an in-orbit power grid. The startup aims to build infrastructure for space-based energy distribution.

title: TrustPoint Receives $4M SpaceWERX Tactical Funding for Independent PNT System
summary: TrustPoint received a $4 million SpaceWERX Tactical Funding Increase (TACFI) contract to develop four satellites and four ground stations for an independent Positioning, Navigation, and Timing (PNT) system demonstration. The funding will support the buildout of a full end-to-end system.

title: Space42 Enhances D2D Prospects with Skylo Partnership
summary: Space42 partnered with Skylo Technologies to enhance direct-to-device (D2D) connectivity using its Thuraya-4 geostationary satellite. This collaboration aims to deliver standards-based D2D connectivity solutions.

title: Vitesse Systems and Accelint Become Part of New Defense Tech Company Lyntris
summary: Vitesse Systems and Accelint have merged to form a new defense technology company named Lyntris, which will focus on the connected battlefield. Both companies were previously owned by Trive Capital.

title: Hughes Posts Decline in Broadband Subscribers and Service Revenue in Q1 2026
summary: Hughes Network Systems reported a decline in broadband subscribers and service revenue for Q1 2026, attributing the drop to competition from rival satellite and terrestrial technologies.

title: Aetherflux Rebrands as Cowboy Space, Expanding Plans to In-Orbit Compute and Launch
summary: Aetherflux, founded by former Robinhood co-founder Baiju Bhatt, has rebranded as Cowboy Space and is expanding its focus to orbital data centers and launch infrastructure.

title: Greek Government Taps Planet for New Satellite Data Deal
summary: The Greek government has signed a 2-year, 7-figure contract with Planet for satellite data services. Planet Labs Germany will provide numerous data solutions under this agreement.

title: Operational AI is Hitting the Limits of Earth Observation Data
summary: AI in Earth Observation is transitioning from experimental use on curated datasets to operational deployment, where models must maintain performance across diverse conditions. This shift highlights the growing challenges in leveraging Earth observation data effectively.
